message:
dlfcn_abi: rename local `native` to `sym` in resolve_main_program_symbol

Small local variable rename inside `resolve_main_program_symbol`:
`native` → `sym`. The name `native` had become misleading — the
pointer the function returns does not have to come from a native /
host-resolved symbol; `resolve_exported_symbol(symbol_name)` also
returns Rust-backed replacement pointers that live in frankenlibc
itself. Calling the local `sym` matches how the rest of the dlfcn
code refers to resolved symbol pointers and removes the implication
that a non-null result is necessarily a host-level handle.

No behavioural change: the is_null check, the returned pointer, and
the RTLD_DEFAULT fallback call into `host_dlsym` are all identical.
This is a readability-only commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
